name: Pumpkin Cognac Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: Finely crushed graham cracker crumbs: 1 1/2 cups
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 10-inch springform pan generously with vegetable shortening.
2. For the crust, mix together graham cracker crumbs, ground almonds, sugar, ginger, cinnamon, and melted butter. Press firmly into the bottom of the greased pan. Set aside.
3. Preheat the oven to 250°F (121°C).
4. For the filling, beat softened cream cheese until smooth in a mixer. Gradually add sugar, beating until light and fluffy. Add cognac, maple syrup, ginger, cinnamon, and nutmeg. Blend well.
5. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in heavy cream and pumpkin. Mix thoroughly.
6. Pour the filling into the unbaked crust and smooth the top. Bake for 2 hours until set but still slightly soft. Check the cake by gently shaking the pan; it should not wiggle.
7. Let the cheesecake cool on a rack for 30 minutes.
8. Increase the oven temperature to 350°F (177°C).
9. For the topping, mix together sour cream, sugar, cognac, and maple syrup until smooth.
10. Spread the topping over the cooled cheesecake. Return to the oven for an additional 7 minutes or until lightly browned on top.
11. Let the cheesecake cool completely at room temperature before removing the sides of the springform pan.
12. Garnish with toasted almond slices around the outer edge.
13. Serve and enjoy! Makes 12 or more servings.
